meta.source: Vanderlande MFD BC216 T5 HBS & Asset Replacement (15000-XX-DS-301-000045)
meta.note: Capacity shares are planning estimates — validate against design throughput data.

hbs.name: HBS 1/2
hbs.role: Primary hold-baggage screening — main check-in (Zones A & B)
downstream: Central sortation → MCA 11–14, HBA 1–9 / 21–24, laterals A1–A16

hbs.name: HBS 3 (North + South)
hbs.role: Screening for Concourse B/C flows, problem bags & make-up laterals C/B/Z
downstream: North & South make-up, laterals C1–C16 / B1–B16 / Z1–Z2, PBA 1–6

hbs.name: HBS 1/3
hbs.role: Transfer baggage screening (Transfer Baggage System / TBS)
downstream: Transfer make-up, Early Bag Store (ISB), re-sortation

MIP.name: Manual Injection Points (MIP)
MIP.desc: Bags hand-loaded onto downstream conveyor to bypass a failed screen/line.
MIP.note: Primary contingency; matches recorded FOH/porterage mitigation volumes (~2–2.9k/day).

XSCREEN.name: Cross-screening reroute
XSCREEN.desc: Divert bags from the failed HBS to an adjacent HBS via Sort/Merge cross-links.
XSCREEN.note: Only where cross_to link exists; consumes spare capacity on the receiving HBS.

OOG.name: OOG / manual-search lane
OOG.desc: Route standard bags through the out-of-gauge manual lane.
OOG.note: Low throughput; last resort for small residuals.

EBS.name: Early Bag Store buffering (ISB)
EBS.desc: Hold bags in ISB/EBS to smooth peaks during partial outage; release when capacity returns.
EBS.note: Time-shifts load; does not add screening capacity.

SHED.name: Check-in throttling / flight hold
SHED.desc: Slow desk acceptance or hold flights to match reduced screening rate.
SHED.note: Passenger-impacting; DAY-A last resort. Coordinated with airline & Duty Manager.

PORTER.name: FOH porterage to stand
PORTER.desc: Direct hand-carry of bags front-of-house to aircraft stand for time-critical flights.
